title: Protect America’s Innovation and Economic Security from CCP Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 607.
summary: The Protect America's Innovation and Economic Security from CCP Act aims to safeguard US innovation and economic interests from potential threats posed by the Chinese Communist Party (CCP). The policy likely entails measures to counter Chinese influence in the US, particularly in areas such as technology and trade.

title: A resolution expressing support for the designation of the month of June 2026 as "National Post-Traumatic Stress Awareness Month" and June 27, 2026, as "National Post-Traumatic Stress Awareness Day".
action: Submitted in the Senate, considered, and agreed to without amendment and with a preamble by Unanimous Consent. (consideration: CR S2670; text: CR S2666)
summary: This resolution supports making June 2026 "National Post-Traumatic Stress Awareness Month" and June 27 a special awareness day. The goal is to draw attention to mental health issues stemming from traumatic experiences.

title: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2026
action: Held at the desk.
summary: The National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2026 outlines plans and funding for military activities in the US for the 2026 financial year. The document aims to provide a framework for the defense budget and policies of the US government for 2026.

title: A bill to expand the mission of the Export-Import Bank of the United States and focus on building export-related domestic critical industries that produce goods and services that support employment in the United States and strengthen global competitiveness, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs. (text: CR S2786-2791; Sponsor introductory remarks on measure: CR S2786)
summary: This bill aims to expand the Export-Import Bank's mission to support domestic industries involved in exporting goods and services. Its goal is to create US employment opportunities and boost global competitiveness.

title: Returning Senate Joint Resolution 3 to the Senate.
action: Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
summary: The Senate Joint Resolution 3 is being returned to the Senate, but it's unclear why. The specifics of this action cannot be determined without further information.

title: Providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Internal Revenue Service relating to "Gross Proceeds Reporting by Brokers That Regularly Provide Services Effectuating Digital Asset Sales".
action: Became Public Law No: 119-5.
summary: This policy aims to allow Congress to disapprove of an IRS rule about how brokers report the proceeds of digital asset sales. The rule reportedly deals with brokers who regularly facilitate sales of digital assets.

title: Proposing an amendment to the Constitution of the United States to prohibit any person who has citizenship or nationality of, or otherwise owes allegiance to, a country other than the United States from serving as a Representative or Senator in Congress, a Judge of the Supreme Court or any inferior court, an Ambassador, public Minister or Consul, or any other officer of the United States which requires the advice and consent of the Senate, or the President or Vice President unless the person formally and permanently relinquishes such citizenship, nationality, or allegiance.
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: This policy proposes amending the US Constitution to limit certain government positions, such as Congress and Supreme Court judges, to US citizens only. The amendment would require individuals with dual citizenship to renounce their non-US allegiance before holding these offices.

title: A joint resolution providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Bureau of Land Management relating to "National Petroleum Reserve in Alaska Integrated Activity Plan Record of Decision".
action: Became Public Law No: 119-47.
summary: This document outlines a US congressional resolution disapproving a rule made by the Bureau of Land Management. The rule pertains to the National Petroleum Reserve in Alaska, an area where oil and gas reserves are managed.

title: Subpoena Abuse Prevention Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Subpoena Abuse Prevention Act is a policy that aims to prevent misuse of subpoenas, which are court orders requiring individuals or organizations to provide evidence or testimony. The details of its implementation and scope are not available.

title: Equitable Access to School Facilities Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 586.
summary: The Equitable Access to School Facilities Act aims to provide fair access to school facilities for all students, regardless of their background or status. The law ensures that schools are inclusive and accommodate the needs of everyone.

title: Critical Minerals Supply Chain Resiliency Act of 2026
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 602.
summary: The Critical Minerals Supply Chain Resiliency Act of 2026 aims to make critical mineral supply chains in the United States more resilient and secure. It is an unidentified law designed to strengthen America's reliance on essential minerals.

title: FLEX Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 566.
summary: The FLEX Act aims to provide a framework for flexible work arrangements, allowing employees to have more control over their work-life balance. The policy is likely to benefit employees by giving them autonomy to manage their time and employers by improving retention and productivity.

title: Empower Charter School Educators to Lead Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 379.
summary: This act aims to empower educators at charter schools to take on leading roles and make better decisions for the education of their students. Its specific provisions and objectives are currently unknown due to lack of available information.

title: Departments of Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2027
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 604.
summary: This document outlines appropriations for the Departments of Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education for the 2027 fiscal year, providing funding for various government programs and agencies. It likely allocates budget allocations and sets spending policies for these departments.

title: A resolution expressing support for the designation of July 2026 as "National Sarcoma Awareness Month".
action: Submitted in the Senate, considered, and agreed to without amendment and with a preamble by Unanimous Consent. (consideration: CR S2705; text: CR S2703)
summary: This document is a resolution from an organization expressing their support to officially recognize July 2026 as a month to raise awareness about sarcoma, a type of cancer. The goal of this recognition is to bring attention to the condition and potentially support further research and education.

title: Military Readiness Permitting Efficiency Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Military Readiness Permitting Efficiency Act of 2026 aims to reduce bureaucratic delays in obtaining permits and approvals for military operations, ensuring a smoother and more efficient process for military readiness. This act seeks to streamline permitting processes to better support national defense efforts.

title: Prior Authorization Reform for Autoimmune and Blood Disorders Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committees on Education and Workforce, and Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Prior Authorization Reform for Autoimmune and Blood Disorders Act aims to simplify the process of getting approval for treatments for autoimmune and blood disorders. This likely involves reducing bureaucratic hurdles or requirements for patients to access necessary medication.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 is aimed at mitigating the negative effects of artificial intelligence on the environment. The act likely includes policies and regulations to minimize AI's ecological footprint.

title: Right to Record Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Right to Record Act of 2026 is a policy aimed at protecting individuals' rights to record and capture information in public spaces. The policy likely outlines laws and regulations surrounding recording devices and their usage in certain environments.

title: Powering the Future of American Space Dominance Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Powering the Future of American Space Dominance Act aims to enhance the United States' space capabilities and position it as a leading power in space exploration and security. The exact details and measures of the act are not specified.
